sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance and key findings
Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for aniline; identification of the most electrophilic (max f⁺) and most nucleophilic (max f⁻) sites.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF).
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied aniline geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); no further optimisation (`DFT//input xyz`).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent; purely gas-phase continuum absent).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, mult 1) for the N-electron state; cation doublet (+1, mult 2) and anion doublet (−1, mult 2) for N±1 states per standard Fukui protocol.
Software: PySCF backend via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (cli invocation recorded below).
Convergence & warnings: All three SCF calculations reached self-consistency; integrity check reports status `ok` and trustworthy = true.  The engine emitted three warnings (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
No other warnings were reported.

Headline result
The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2) with f⁺ = 2.2975867.
(The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (N) with f⁻ = 0.20452359.)

Reproducibility
Live log (written during the run): fukui_<timestamp>.out in the current working directory (you can `tail -f` it for full SCF and population-analysis details).
CLI command: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpnjhx5vzk/live.json
Plot of atom-mapped reactivity indices: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_o3/20260702-200026_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
The full JSON result (including per-atom charges and Fukui vectors) is saved at the `--out` path above.

Note: The Fukui functions are Mulliken-charge based; absolute magnitudes depend on population scheme. Relative trends across atoms are generally robust, but absolute values are sensitive to the partitioning method.
